name: harbor
title: Harbor Blvd (Fullerton → Santa Ana)
route_mi_note: corridor length, miles (scripts/route43_share.py, scripts/anchor_from_apc.py: Route 43 runs ~18 mi but the priced corridor is 12.1). Consumed by model.py's design-sweep fleet annotation (capcost.fleet) and matches the tbc harbor profile / capcost gate route length.
anchor_note: MEASURED (2026-07): OCTA quarterly performance reports give FY2019 route totals 43 = 2,095,510 and 543 = 953,471 (scripts/anchor_from_apc.py, source URLs inside). Weekday: 543 (wkdy-only) 3,739; 43 (7-day) 6,350-6,985; x FY2019->FY2024 system trend 0.90-0.99 (central 0.94); corridor = 543 + 43 x corridor share 0.75 (LODES) - 0.86 (ACS), scripts/route43_share.py. 43+543 = 8.3-8.7% of system FY2017-20, matching the TSP study's '8%' quote (which this data supersedes). Cross-check: 12,800 on-Harbor boardings in 2015 (Central Harbor study) x trend x street-vs-corridor share ~ 8,400.

anchor_derivation.note: spec 08 A2b: the FY2019->FY2024 system trend and the Route-43 corridor share, promoted from anchor_note prose to structured keys (assumptions registry entries anchor_trend / corr_share). anchor_low/high (7650/9650) cross-check against the anchor_from_apc.py product within rounding-to-50. corr_share is the SAME assumption the 2013 backtest anchor cites (config/backtest_543.json: route43_total x corr_share -> 9750/11180); one entry, two citing derivations (spec 08 §2).

rapid.note: Route 543 per corridor doc; GTFS current = 12.8 mph / 20 min (sensitivity row)

service_new.note: 5-min peak / 10-min off-peak operating plan (user decision 2026-07); flat 5-min all-day kept as sensitivity row, and sub-5-min (3.5/7, 2.5/5) headways swept per the owner 2026-07-17 frequency test (GoA4 makes them cheap to run). Base routes stay scalar: GTFS measures 43 and 543 at flat 20-min peak AND midday. speed 30.0 is the EXOGENOUS FALLBACK, retained as the historical governance toggle (exogenous_speed=1, reproduces the pre-derived-speed headline); the headline now DERIVES average speed from the v_cruise/dwell priors and this spacing (spec 02 §4.9, grade-separated variant): owner design cruise 60 mph (v_cruise central 96.6 km/h) / 25 s dwell / 1.0-mi -> ~31.8 mph derived (was ~30 mph at the old 80 km/h literature central)

visitor.note: Anaheim resort/visitor market: share of base boardings (pinned like tau); short trips (hotel-Disneyland-convention); random arrival (h/2 wait, no schedule adaptation). Judgment call: old first bin 0.55 split 0.25/0.30 into the new 0-0.5-mi bin -- visitor trips are exactly the sub-half-mile kind

bca.rev_hours_note: Weekday revenue vehicle-hours for the BCA's avoided base O&M (spec 06 E4): sum over one weekday's trips of (last-stop arrival - first-stop departure), OCTA GTFS in data/raw/gtfs. Weekday = the calendar.monday=1 services (both routes run their weekday trips under service_id MTUWTF): route 43 = 114 trips -> 183.3 h, route 543 = 86 trips -> 77.1 h. Fold sheds both the local 43 and the rapid 543; retain keeps the local 43 (only the 543 is folded into the new line).
